mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5%, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that typically precede corporate earnings downgrades, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite UNRATE exceeding 4.5%, or the signal's predictive power vanishes in post-2008 data.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand shock that overwhelms supply-side resilience, leading to negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: The interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling 3-year regression, or SPX forward returns do not systematically decline when both proxies fall simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, aggressive federal funds rate hikes compress equity multiples by raising real discount rates, causing negative forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly negative when CPIAUCSL exceeds 315 and FEDFUNDS rises concurrently, or the signal flips to positive predictive power in the same regime.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real disposable income expands consumer spending capacity, driving corporate revenue growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC for RSAFS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no monotonic relationship with RSAFS growth rates.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in payroll employment exceeding 200,000 per month signals robust economic momentum that sustains corporate profit margins, yielding positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict positive 20-day returns in > 55% of triggered months, or the predictive relationship breaks down during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong industrial output and weak GDP growth indicates supply-side bottlenecks that squeeze corporate margins, leading to negative equity returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling 2-year window, or SPX forward returns do not decline when INDPRO outpaces GDPC1 growth.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated unemployment combined with stagnant federal funds rates, the labor market signals a policy-induced floor that supports equity valuations, producing positive returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are not significantly positive when both UNRATE exceeds 4.0% and FEDFUNDS remains below 4.5%, or the signal consistently predicts negative returns in this regime.
